title: amount_zatoshi cast u64→i64 in upsert_session: negative value in SQLite for extreme inputs
description: upsert_session binds session.amount_zatoshi as i64 (.bind(session.amount_zatoshi as i64)). For amounts above i64::MAX zatoshi (~92 billion ZEC), the cast silently wraps to negative and SQLite stores a negative integer. all_sessions() reads it back as i64 and casts to u64, so the roundtrip is lossless, but any SQL inspection or future WHERE amount > 0 query would see a negative value. In practice Zcash total supply is 21M ZEC so this is unreachable via /pay, but there is no assertion enforcing the bound. Trigger: construct a PaymentSession with amount_zatoshi > i64::MAX (possible via direct API, not via CLI). Fix: use i64 throughout or assert amount_zatoshi <= i64::MAX as i64 before casting.
